---
title: "Distributional regression for biological data"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Distributional regression for biological data}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(collapse = TRUE, comment = "#>")
```

`drmTMB` currently implements Gaussian location-scale models with fixed effects
and optional random intercepts in the location formula:

```r
drmTMB(
  bf(y ~ x1 + (1 | id), sigma ~ x1),
  family = gaussian(),
  data = dat
)
```

The first implemented bivariate seed covers fixed-effect location-coscale
regression with predictor-dependent residual correlation:

```r
drmTMB(
  bf(
    mu1 = y1 ~ x1 + x2,
    mu2 = y2 ~ x1,
    sigma1 = ~ x1 + x2,
    sigma2 = ~ x1,
    rho12 = ~ x1 + x2
  ),
  family = biv_gaussian(),
  data = dat
)
```

The planned O'Dea-style version adds the individual-level random intercept and
random slope structure to each mean formula:

```r
drmTMB(
  formula = drm_formula(
    mu1 = y1 ~ x1 + x2 + (1 + x2 | p | ID),
    mu2 = y2 ~ x1      + (1 + x2 | p | ID),
    sigma1 = ~ x1 + x2,
    sigma2 = ~ x1,
    rho12 = ~ x1 + x2
  ),
  family = c(gaussian(), gaussian()),
  data = dat
)
```

Here `sigma1` and `sigma2` are residual scale parameters. The random-effect
standard deviations and correlations from `(1 + x2 | p | ID)` are separate
group-level quantities.

Meta-analysis uses the ordinary Gaussian family plus a known sampling
variance marker:

```r
drmTMB(
	  bf(
	    yi ~ x1 + x2 + meta_known_V(V = V),
	    sigma ~ x1
	  ),
  family = gaussian(),
  data = dat
)
```
